title: Generative QA
concept: Generative QA synthesizes a new answer sentence based on the context, using decoder models to explain details rather than just cropping them.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of Generative QA when answering a question?
exercises.explain: Generative QA synthesizes natural language answers from scratch rather than just extracting existing phrases.

cards.title: Beyond Copy-Paste Answers
cards.body: Have you ever asked a smart assistant a question, and instead of just reading out a random web snippet, it explained the answer to you in a complete, friendly sentence? That's the power of Generative QA!

cards.title: What is Generative QA?
cards.body: Generative QA is a technology where an AI reads a question and some context, and then writes a brand new answer sentence from scratch. Instead of just copying and pasting a snippet of text, it uses a decoder model to synthesize a natural, custom explanation.

cards.title: The Helpful Teacher
cards.body: Imagine asking a history teacher a question. Instead of just pointing their finger at a specific sentence in a textbook (Extractive QA), they read the page, process it, and explain it to you in their own friendly words. Generative QA is that helpful teacher!

cards.title: Generative QA in Action
cards.body:
If you ask "Why is the sky blue?" and give the AI a science page:

• Extractive QA might just highlight: "scattering of light"
• Generative QA writes: "The sky looks blue because the air scatters blue sunlight more than other colors."

cards.title: Not Just a Copy Machine
cards.body: People often think Generative QA just copies the best sentence from a document. In reality, it doesn't copy-paste at all! It uses a decoder to generate completely new sentences word by word, translating the facts into a fresh explanation.

cards.title: Extractive vs. Generative
cards.body: Let's compare how the two QA approaches handle answering questions from a document.

left.text: Crops out and highlights an exact snippet directly from the text.

right.text: Synthesizes a brand new, natural sentence to explain the answer.

exercises.prompt: What makes Generative QA different from Extractive QA?

options.text: It crops out the exact words from the source text.
It synthesizes a new answer sentence based on the context.
It only works with numbers and math equations.
It translates words into different languages without reading them.

exercises.explain: Generative QA models synthesize and write new sentences to answer questions, whereas Extractive QA only crops out existing snippets of text.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Generative QA can write brand new sentences that do not appear in the original source document.
exercises.explain: Generative QA uses a decoder to construct and write entirely new sentences to explain concepts.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing component used in Generative QA to synthesize answers:
exercises.sentence: Generative QA uses a [blank] model to write a fresh response instead of just cropping text.
exercises.explain: Generative QA systems rely on decoder models to generate new sequences of text word-by-word, rather than just highlighting existing text.

exercises.prompt: Which of these responses looks like it was created by a Generative QA system?

options.text: "Venus is the hottest planet because its thick atmosphere traps heat like a greenhouse."
"hottest planet in our solar system is Venus"

exercises.explain: The first option is generated by an AI assistant summarizing the info. The second is an exact cropped snippet (Extractive). The third is just search keywords.

exercises.prompt: You want to build a system that explains complex science topics to kids in simple terms using a textbook. Why would you choose Generative QA over Extractive QA?

options.text: It extracts exact words from the document without changing them.
It writes a brand new, synthesized explanation based on the text.
It only answers with simple 'yes' or 'no' statements.
It searches the internet for images related to the question.

exercises.explain: Generative QA models act like writers, generating brand new sentences to explain things, whereas Extractive QA models act like highlighters, only copying text exactly as it appears.
